title: Museum Mania Day Around the World

story: Zap! Lightning flashes across the sky. But did you know there's an invisible layer up there that helps radio waves travel? Oliver Heaviside, born in London on May 18, 1850, predicted this special layer called the ionosphere. This brilliant English physicist's work helps us communicate across long distances today!

title: A Nobel Scientist's Birth
story: Vincent du Vigneaud, a brilliant biochemist, was born in Chicago on May 18, 1901. His groundbreaking work on oxytocin, a hormone that plays a crucial role in childbirth and bonding, earned him the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1955. Du Vigneaud's discoveries helped scientists better understand the human body's amazing abilities.

title: Midnight Oil's Musical Spark Ignites

title: Germany's Money Merger
